# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: External Contribution CI

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # `pull_request_target` runs in the context of the base repository.
          # To access the PR's head commit from the contributor's fork securely,
          # we explicitly specify the repository and ref from the event payload.
          repository: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          # The `GITHUB_TOKEN` provided by `pull_request_target` has read-only
          # permissions to the *forked* repository.
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup environment (Optional: e.g., Node.js, Python, Java)
        # Uncomment and configure for your specific project needs.
        # Example for Node.js:
        # uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        # with:
        #   node-version: '18'
        # Example for Python:
        # uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        # with:
        #   python-version: '3.9'
        run: |
          echo "--- Setting up environment (if required) ---"
          # Add any environment setup commands here, e.g., install specific tools.
          echo "Environment setup complete (simulated)."

      - name: Install dependencies
        id: install_deps
        run: |
          echo "--- Installing project dependencies ---"
          # Replace with your project's actual dependency installation commands.
          # Examples:
          # npm install
          # pip install -r requirements.txt
          # composer install
          # go mod download
          # mvn install -DskipTests
          echo "Dependencies installed successfully (simulated)."
          # If installation fails, use `exit 1` to fail this step
          # exit 1

      - name: Run tests
        id: run_tests
        # Ensure tests only run if dependency installation was successful.
        if: steps.install_deps.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          echo "--- Running project tests ---"
          # Replace with your project's actual test commands.
          # Examples:
          # npm test
          # pytest
          # go test ./...
          # phpunit
          # mvn test
          echo "Tests completed successfully (simulated)."
          # To simulate a test failure for demonstration:
          # exit 1

      - name: Post test results as a comment
        # This step will always run, whether previous steps succeeded or failed,
        # ensuring feedback is always provided on the pull request.
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const prNumber = context.issue.number;
            if (!prNumber) {
              console.log('Not a pull request context. Skipping comment.');
              return;
            }

            const installDepsOutcome = steps.install_deps.outcome;
            const runTestsOutcome = steps.run_tests.outcome;

            let commentBody = '';
            let emoji = '';
            let overallStatus = 'Unknown Status';

            if (installDepsOutcome === 'failure') {
              emoji = '⚠️';
              overallStatus = 'Dependency Installation Failed';
              commentBody = `### ${emoji} ${overallStatus}\n\n`;
              commentBody += 'Failed to install project dependencies. Please review the workflow logs for details on the failure.';
            } else if (runTestsOutcome === 'success') {
              emoji = '✅';
              overallStatus = 'Build and Test Successful';
              commentBody = `### ${emoji} ${overallStatus}\n\n`;
              commentBody += 'All automated checks passed for this pull request. Thank you for your contribution!';
            } else if (runTestsOutcome === 'failure') {
              emoji = '❌';
              overallStatus = 'Build and Test Failed';
              commentBody = `### ${emoji} ${overallStatus}\n\n`;
              commentBody += 'Some automated checks failed for this pull request. Please review the workflow logs for details and address the issues.';
            } else {
              emoji = '❓';
              overallStatus = 'Build and Test Status Unclear';
              commentBody = `### ${emoji} ${overallStatus}\n\n`;
              commentBody += 'The status of automated checks could not be fully determined. Please review the workflow logs for more information.';
            }

            commentBody += `\n\n_Workflow run: [${context.workflow} #${context.runNumber}](${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId})_`;

            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: prNumber,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: commentBody
            });
